title: 3rd Impact and You
wikitext:
[]: 3rd Impact and You             Quote Post by n00dle » Sun Jun 10, 2007 1:49 pm It's a normal day and your busy walking the dog, or working on your garden, or inside posting things on Eva Monkey, or at the office filling out PTS Forms, and it happens: You hear this strange noise....you look up...and to your horror see a massive naked girl in the sky, and a wave of light and ascending cross's heading towards your. :shock: Before you can react however, it reaches you, and everything goes dark. You then find yourself in the sea of mind fuck. What do you do? Would you return, or would you give in and accept immortality and nothingness? I can say with almost 100% certainty that I would not be coming back. My misanthropy would basically guarantee my preference to spending eternity as Tang rather than coming back to spend another second with humans. Anyway, what about you all? What choice would you make?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Jun 10, 2007 1:58 pm I'd come back after a while. Im genraly a good guy I just take a while to make up my mind some times. As for what I'd see in their not a fucking clue

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Sun Jun 10, 2007 2:00 pm I supposedly believe that nothing is perfect in this world and that eternal happiness is an absurd idea, so I'd probably reject it. Besides, I already know what third impact is and wouldn't like to spend eternity as tang. =P

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jun 10, 2007 2:08 pm Provided Shinji-kun already did the rejecting for me and its up to each person to "imagine themselves in their own heart", I'd like to think that I *WOULD* come back, but to be frank, it'd probably take me 2 months or so like it did with Asuka. -_-

[]:             Quote Post by n00dle » Sun Jun 10, 2007 4:14 pm I supposedly believe that nothing is perfect in this world and that eternal happiness is an absurd idea

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Sun Jun 10, 2007 4:23 pm Well, from the images people saw before they tang, I think its safe to assume that these people were willing to go along with 3rd impact because of the allure of being with the one you love. I'm trying to say that while 3rd impact isn't happiness, you kinda get tricked into believing that it is.

[]: Re: 3rd Impact and You             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sun Jun 10, 2007 4:28 pm n00dle wrote: You then find yourself in the sea of mind fuck. What do you do? Would you return, or would you give in and accept immortality and nothingness?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jun 10, 2007 4:31 pm ....I think my cats Timmy & Butters (Yes, that's their names ^^; ) would appear to me. :P *Rei messenger turns into Timmy & Butters: Purr purr purr! Meow?* "Hey, guys, I'm so happy to see---" TANGED!

[]:             Quote Post by n00dle » Sun Jun 10, 2007 4:58 pm Well, I don't think 3rd Impact tricks anyone. Fuyutsuki was certainly aware of what it was, and wanted it to fail so I don't think it would make sense for him to be tricked at the end. Regardless, seems most of you figure you would return, but I feel that's making to light of the matter. It's easy to say that now, but think real hard. If it really did happen would you really want to return to a world of pain and misery? Maybe I should put it another way: what if you were actually living in 2015, and Eva as a show didn't exist, and you had no inside knowledge of any of this? If you were one of 3 billion people totally ignorant of Seele or their plans, what would you choose then? The thing with 3I is that Shinji, who appears weak most of the time, actually proves the strongest in the end when he says "no", and rejects Thanatos (the death instinct, the natural human urge to return to a lower existence). The fact that other than him, we see only Asuka return shows just how hard it is. You have two choices: return to a world which is broken, full of sorrow, and, after 3I, may never again contain any of your friends or family. You may be that only one in your whole town who decides to come back. Or choose eternal peace, basically death though it's called eternal life. I know I'm going on and on, but 3I is the single biggest event in Eva, and the goal that everything is geared towards. Don't shrug it off so easily.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Jun 10, 2007 5:01 pm I would probably go back and forth from reality to Tang and vice versa depending on my mood, really.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jun 10, 2007 5:19 pm I think you can guess my answer from previous 3I threads...

[]:             Quote Post by n00dle » Sun Jun 10, 2007 6:43 pm I can't be bothered to look through a bunch of 3I threads right now, care to give me the cliff notes version?

[]:             Quote Post by Hailtheplatypus » Sun Jun 10, 2007 7:50 pm I would have caused it :shock: but seriously... I would prolly stay as Tang for a while and plot, after figuring out how I wanted it to work I would return, and cause Forth Impact :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Sun Jun 10, 2007 9:31 pm This would be a tough one... but if I had to choose, I'd come back. I'd rather be hurt than to be "non-existant" per se

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Sun Jun 10, 2007 10:27 pm I'd check the boundaries of Tang-dom before making my decision. I'd end up coming back anyway, but what I can do there will determine my stay time.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jun 11, 2007 12:03 am well in nothingness, it would be kinda boring after like 2 min, so i would definently come back, i got too much to live for

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Mon Jun 11, 2007 12:20 am Fuck being Tanged, I'd rather be judged on my merits as a person. The idea of getting turned into a giant puddle in the middle of post-apocaliptic Japan does not thrill me in any measure of the word. Eva-verse is a place I'd rathr not live in...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jun 11, 2007 1:22 am n00dle wrote: care to give me the cliff notes version?

[]:             Quote Post by n00dle » Mon Jun 11, 2007 1:41 am Wow...and I thought I was ruthless and pessimistic...end up as fuck toys? Cheerful...anyway, that doesn't really explain if you'd come back, it just explains what you think it would be like for those two. I also disagree that the ones who return first will be opportunity seekers. Many of those kinds of people are fueled purely by hatred and malice. I doubt many of them would survive after coming face to face with their inner selves. And after seeing how many of who (read: all of the responses so far) claim you would return...I dunno what to think really. I still feel you guys are missing the point. Instrumentality would force all of us to see what we really are deep down inside, strip us down to our bare components as it were. I understand everyone is probably pretty happy with themselves but what I think Anno was trying to get across is that everyone has issues, even if they don't realize it. And when confronted with those problems, we may not like what we see so much we can't take it. This is starting to venture into post-3I talk...ah hell, who cares? I'll just run with it. Looking at other threads on this topic, I've noticed a lot of people assume that sooner or later a large portion of the population would return. I honestly don't get this. I'd say that maybe, MAYBE, 25-50 million people out of 3 billion would eventually return. See paragraph above for my reasons. I think a large number of people would simply say yes to being tang. Being one with their loved ones for ever and all.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Mon Jun 11, 2007 2:40 am Well, seeing myself as I really am, I know I wouldn't come back for a long time. I've suffered a lot of pain and depression in this life as it is, and I'd probably be overcome by this for a while. Not quite sure what I'd do while I was in there, but I hope that I wouldn't attempt to cover up my problems by using more...carnal means. But you know, after a while, I would try to collect myself, as in reality. I like to spend time alone to regather myself. Where is my will power? Where is my self in all of this? And I'd think "Sure, its a nice return, but how does this help the greater good? Our evolution?" In a way, Third Impact has a huge benefit for humanity for those who wish to see this: realizing that we can all universally understand each other. It's just that fear of pain and being rejected by others that drive us back, but this pain and rejection comes from focusing on others so much. Notice how in the series, most of the characters are out for themselves and only care for themselves. One notable exception to this is Shinji, which is why I identify with him the most (I think that people identify him as selfish because of how much time he thinks and cares about others, making him appear introverted, thus "selfish." But this is exactly why he suffers from pain. He thinks about people, but does nothing to better his relationships because he's so afraid to cause pain for others, because their pain becomes his. But anyway...). And sure, this false reality is an ideal, for humanity to universally understand each other so that our hearts can suffer no more pain from selfish individualization. But this isn't done artificially. It is done in real time, brick for brick, and not using a proxy method aka Lilith. So in the end, I would remain in Third Impact for some time while I sorted out my problems, but I think I would eventually recognize what the false reality really is: a cop out.

You may be that only one in your whole town who decides to come back. Or choose eternal peace, basically death though it's called eternal life. I know I'm going on and on, but 3I is the single biggest event in Eva, and the goal that everything is geared towards. Don't shrug it off so easily.
